briefing: Announce the Colab MCP Server, enabling connections between any AI agent and Google Colab.

articles.title: Plan mode is now available in Gemini CLI
articles.snippet: Gemini CLI now features Plan Mode, a read-only environment that allows the AI to analyze complex codebases and map out architectural changes without the risk of accidental execution. By leveraging the new ask_user tool and expanded Model Context Protocol (MCP) support, developers can collaboratively refine strategies and pull in external data before committing to implementation.
articles.ai_summary: Gemini CLI has introduced Plan Mode, a read-only feature that allows developers to analyze codebases and plan architectural changes without executing code. This mode enhances collaboration and reduces the risk of errors during development.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances developer safety and collaboration in AI-driven coding environments.

articles.title: Announcing the Colab MCP Server: Connect Any AI Agent to Google Colab
articles.snippet: When you’re prototyping locally with AI agents like Gemini CLI, Claude Code, or your own agent, thei...
articles.ai_summary: The Colab MCP Server has been launched, enabling AI agents like Gemini CLI and Claude Code to connect seamlessly with Google Colab for local prototyping. This integration simplifies the development process for AI applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Streamlines AI development by integrating powerful tools in a collaborative environment.

articles.title: Developer’s Guide to AI Agent Protocols
articles.snippet: This blog post introduces a suite of six protocols, such as MCP and A2A, designed to eliminate custom integration code by standardizing how AI agents access data and communicate. Using a "kitchen manager" agent as a practical example, it demonstrates how these tools handle complex tasks like real-time inventory checks, wholesale commerce via UCP, and secure payment authorization through AP2. By leveraging the Agent Development Kit (ADK), developers can also implement A2UI and AG-UI to deliver in
articles.ai_summary: A new guide introduces six AI agent protocols, including MCP and A2A, aimed at standardizing data access and communication for developers. The guide uses a kitchen manager agent example to illustrate practical applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Standardizes AI agent communication, making development more efficient and less error-prone.

articles.title: Build a smart financial assistant with LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1
articles.snippet: This blog post introduces a workflow for extracting high-quality data from complex, unstructured documents by combining LlamaParse with Gemini 3.1 models. It demonstrates an event-driven architecture that uses Gemini 3.1 Pro for agentic parsing of dense financial tables and Gemini 3.1 Flash for cost-effective summarization. By following the provided tutorial, developers can build a personal finance assistant capable of transforming messy brokerage statements into structured, human-readable insig
articles.ai_summary: A tutorial demonstrates how to build a financial assistant using LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1, focusing on extracting data from unstructured documents. The workflow aims to improve the processing of complex financial information.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers developers to create advanced financial tools that enhance data usability.

articles.title: Jump to play: Building with Gemini & MediaPipe
articles.snippet: The provided workflow streamlines motion-controlled game development by using Gemini Canvas to rapidly prototype mechanics like the MediaPipe Pose Landmarker through high-level prompting. Developers can refine these prototypes in Google AI Studio by optimizing for low-latency "lite" models and stable tracking points, such as shoulder landmarks, to ensure responsive gameplay. The process concludes by using Gemini Code Assist to refactor experimental code into a modular, production-ready applicati
articles.ai_summary: This article outlines a workflow for developing motion-controlled games using Gemini and MediaPipe, emphasizing rapid prototyping and optimization for responsive gameplay. It highlights the importance of refining game mechanics effectively.
articles.why_it_matters: Facilitates innovative game development by leveraging AI for real-time interaction.

articles.title: Closing the knowledge gap with agent skills
articles.snippet: To bridge the gap between static model knowledge and rapidly evolving software practices, Google DeepMind developed a "Gemini API developer skill" that provides agents with live documentation and SDK guidance. Evaluation results show a massive performance boost, with the gemini-3.1-pro-preview model jumping from a 28.2% to a 96.6% success rate when equipped with the skill. This lightweight approach demonstrates how giving models strong reasoning capabilities and access to a "source of truth" can
articles.ai_summary: Google DeepMind has developed a 'Gemini API developer skill' to enhance AI agents' performance by providing live documentation and SDK guidance, significantly improving success rates in tasks. This approach bridges knowledge gaps in evolving software practices.
articles.why_it_matters: Boosts AI agent effectiveness by integrating real-time learning and documentation.

articles.title: Announcing ADK for Java 1.0.0: Building the Future of AI Agents in Java
articles.snippet: Google has released version 1.0.0 of the Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Java, introducing powerful new features like Google Maps grounding, built-in URL fetching, and a standardized Agent2Agent protocol for cross-framework collaboration. The update enhances agent control through a new "App" and "Plugin" architecture, which allows for global logging, automated context window management via event compaction, and "Human-in-the-Loop" workflows for action confirmations. Additionally, the release pro
articles.ai_summary: Google has released version 1.0.0 of the Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Java, featuring new capabilities for agent control and collaboration across frameworks. The update aims to enhance the development of AI agents in Java.
articles.why_it_matters: Strengthens Java's role in AI development with improved tools for agent collaboration.

articles.snippet: Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) middleware for LangChain — self-improving playbooks from the ICLR 2026 paper by Zhang et al.
articles.ai_summary: The agent-episodic-memory package added to PyPI introduces middleware for LangChain, enabling self-improving playbooks based on recent research. This could enhance the adaptability of AI agents.
articles.why_it_matters: Advances the capabilities of AI agents through innovative engineering.

articles.title: mcp-bastion-mistral 0.1.0
articles.snippet: MCP-Bastion security middleware for Mistral AI — prompt injection, PII, and rate-limit protection
articles.ai_summary: MCP-Bastion 0.1.0 is a new security middleware for Mistral AI, providing protections against prompt injection, PII leaks, and rate limits. This release aims to enhance the security of AI applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Addresses critical security concerns in AI deployment, ensuring safer applications.

articles.title: $1.6 billion Ether Machine SPAC deal collapses over unfavorable market
articles.snippet: The Ether Machine, an ether treasury firm, currently has more than $1 billion in ether in its treasury.

articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the volatility in the crypto market and its effects on large financial deals.

articles.title: Top Stories: iPhone Rumors, Apple's MacBook Neo Dilemma, and More

articles.title: XChat: Meet X’s Rival To WhatsApp, Telegram And iMessage
articles.snippet: X (formerly Twitter) is preparing to launch a new standalone messaging app called XChat for iPhone and iPad as part of its broader strategy to evolve into a “super app” offering multiple integrated services. The app is currently available for pre-registration…
articles.ai_summary: X is launching XChat, a new messaging app aimed at competing with WhatsApp, Telegram, and iMessage, as part of its evolution into a super app.
